perturbation.original_text: 4.1.1. During the Term, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for the sale of ***** of the third party advertising inventory (which shall consist of a minimum of one advertisement per page on each of the "Co-Branded Equipment," "Co-Branded Careers," "Resources" and "Home Page" sections or successor, replacement or substitute sections) of the PaperExchange Site and shall be consistent with the amount of advertising on other business to business vertical sites on the PaperExchange Site (the "Third Party Advertising Allocation"). PaperExchange shall retain the right to place advertisements for its own account on the remaining ***** of the Third Party Advertising Allocation; provided, however, that if any portion of such Third Party Advertising Allocation remains unsold 45 days after it becomes available for advertising, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for third party advertising on such unsold Third Party Advertising Allocation.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.1. During the Term, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for the sale of ***** of the third party advertising inventory (which shall consist of a minimum of one advertisement per page on each of the "Co-Branded Equipment," "Co-Branded Careers," "Resources" and "Home Page" sections or successor, replacement or substitute sections) of the PaperExchange Site and shall be consistent with the amount of advertising on other business to business vertical sites on the PaperExchange Site (the "Third Party Advertising Allocation"). PaperExchange shall retain the right to place advertisements for its own account on the remaining ***** of the Third Party Advertising Allocation; provided, however, that if any portion of such Third Party Advertising Allocation remains unsold 15 days after it becomes available for advertising, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for third party advertising on such unsold Third Party Advertising Allocation.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the period after which VerticalNet can arrange for advertising on unsold inventory from '45 days' to '15 days'. This contradicts the original agreement and creates uncertainty about when VerticalNet gains the right to sell the unsold advertising space on PaperExchange Site.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.1. During the Term, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for the sale of ***** of the third party advertising inventory (which shall consist of a minimum of one advertisement per page on each of the "Co-Branded Equipment," "Co-Branded Careers," "Resources" and "Home Page" sections or successor, replacement or substitute sections) of the PaperExchange Site and shall be consistent with the amount of advertising on other business to business vertical sites on the PaperExchange Site (the "Third Party Advertising Allocation"). PaperExchange shall retain the right to place advertisements for its own account on the remaining ***** of the Third Party Advertising Allocation; provided, however, that if any portion of such Third Party Advertising Allocation remains unsold 15 days after it becomes available for advertising, VerticalNet shall have the exclusive right to arrange for third party advertising on such unsold Third Party Advertising Allocation.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the period after which VerticalNet can arrange for advertising on unsold inventory from '45 days' to '15 days'. The contradicted text states the period is 15 days. Since the original text said 45 days and the contradicted text says 15 days, and the change now states 15 days, the contradicted text is in direct conflict with the original text, causing a contradiction. The inconsistency in deadlines creates uncertainty regarding which term is enforced, and therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 7.5. Payment Terms. Each party shall provide the other party with all amounts due under this Agreement for the prior calendar quarter within 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter during the Term. Each payment shall be accompanied by a statement detailing the amount of applicable gross revenue received, the calculation of the amount due to the other party and the amount of the payment accompanying such statement. All payments due to either party hereunder shall be made in immediately available U.S. funds, without set-off or counterclaim, free and clear of (and without deduction for or grossed up for, as applicable), any taxes, duties, charges, withholdings, restrictions or conditions of any nature imposed or levied by any governmental taxing or other authority.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.5. Payment Terms. Each party shall provide the other party with all amounts due under this Agreement for the prior calendar quarter within 60 days after the end of each calendar quarter during the Term. Each payment shall be accompanied by a statement detailing the amount of applicable gross revenue received, the calculation of the amount due to the other party and the amount of the payment accompanying such statement. All payments due to either party hereunder shall be made in immediately available U.S. funds, without set-off or counterclaim, free and clear of (and without deduction for or grossed up for, as applicable), any taxes, duties, charges, withholdings, restrictions or conditions of any nature imposed or levied by any governmental taxing or other authority.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction, because Section 8.2 states termination can occur after 30 days in case of breach of payment obligation. Changing section 7.5 to 60 days, contradict Section 8.2 where it specifies a 30-day cure period for breach of a payment obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2. Termination for Cause. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other party in the event any material breach of a material term of this Agreement by such other party that remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other party; provided, however that if such breach is not reasonably capable of cure within the applicable cure period, the breaching party shall have an additional 180 days to cure such breach so long as the cure is commenced within the applicable cure period and thereafter is diligently prosecuted to completion as soon as possible.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7.5, extending the payment period from 30 to 60 days. This directly contradicts Section 8.2, which specifies a 30-day cure period for breach of a payment obligation. The change in 7.5 effectively makes it impossible to comply with the cure period outlined in 8.2, creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the time allowed to rectify a payment breach. The definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction applies in this instance as 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines', making the text contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1. Automatic Renewal. This Agreement will automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term or a subsequent renewal term on a year to year basis (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless either party notifies the other at least 30 days prior to the end of the Initial Term or then current Renewal Term, as applicable, of its intention not to renew this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1. Automatic Renewal. This Agreement will automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term or a subsequent renewal term on a year to year basis (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless either party notifies the other at least 90 days prior to the end of the Initial Term or then current Renewal Term, as applicable, of its intention not to renew this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The Initial Term definition 1.9 specifies that the initial term is four years from the effective date. Then, because the contract automatically renews, this modification changes the notification period required not to renew this Agreement from 30 days to 90 days, potentially conflicting with expectations based on the original term length and creating uncertainty about the actual termination requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.9. Initial Term shall mean the Effective Date through the day prior to the fourth anniversary of the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 8.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 8.1 changes the notification period for non-renewal from 30 days to 90 days. This directly contradicts Section 1.9, which defines the initial term as four years and implies a certain expectation for termination procedures. The increase in the notification period creates uncertainty about the actual termination requirements after the initial term, as it might not align with the original expectations based on the four-year term and previous 30-day notice. The new 90 day notice period directly conflicts with the reasonable expectation set by the initial four year term when reading the original document. This falls under the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.
